name: RESTOR is recruiting older adults for rapamycin and everolimus PK/PD with no posted results
summary: RESTOR repairs active trial coverage for Rapalogs by tracking a direct rapamycin/everolimus older-adult PK/PD study, but it is mechanistic no-results context rather than efficacy evidence.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT06658093 as a recruiting 194-participant early phase 1 PK/PD study of rapamycin and everolimus in healthy older adults, measuring blood, adipose, and muscle drug levels and mTOR signaling endpoints with no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
population_or_model: Healthy community-dwelling adults aged 65 to 90 years
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 194; estimated primary and study completion are both July 2028; results were not posted.
caveats: Recruiting registry status is not evidence of benefit.
The trial is primarily PK/PD and basic-science context.
Target engagement in blood, adipose, or muscle would still need connection to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
